content:
2 min read
1 hour ago

--

| Scenario | Recommended |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------- |
| Small Angular App | Signal Store |
| Medium Enterprise App | Signal Store |
| Large Enterprise App (Existing NgRx) | NgRx Store |
| Local Component State | Component Store / Signals |
| Angular 19+ New Project | Signal Store |
| Complex Event-Driven Architecture | NgRx Store |NgRx Store (Redux Style)
Architecture
Action
↓
Reducer
↓
Store
↓
Selector
↓
ComponentExample:
store.dispatch(loadUsers());
store.select(selectUsers);Pros
✅ Enterprise standard
✅ Time travel debugging
✅ Predictable state changes
✅ Excellent DevTools
Cons
❌ Lots of boilerplate
Action
Reducer
Effect
Selector
FacadeToo much code for small features.
Component Store
Introduced to solve local state complexity.
Architecture
Component
↓
ComponentStoreExample
@Injectable()
export class UserStore extends ComponentStore<State> {
readonly users$ = this.select(state => state.users);
readonly loadUsers = this.effect(() => {
...
});
}Pros
✅ Less boilerplate
✅ Feature-level state
✅ RxJS based
Cons
❌ Still RxJS heavy
❌ Need subscriptions
❌ More operators
switchMap
tap
combineLatest
withLatestFromNgRx Signal Store
Newest approach.
Architecture
Signal Store
↓
Signals
↓
ComponentExample
export const UserStore = signalStore(
withState({
users: []
}),
withMethods((store) => ({
loadUsers() {
...
}
}))
);Read State
store.users();instead of
store.select(selectUsers);Computed State
withComputed((store) => ({
totalUsers: computed(
() => store.users().length
)
}));Pros
✅ Minimal boilerplate
✅ No selectors
✅ No subscriptions
✅ Faster change detection
✅ Angular Signals integration
✅ Easier to learn
Cons
❌ New ecosystem
❌ DevTools not as mature
❌ Some advanced Redux patterns still easier in Store
